new_classes.definition_en: A quality of a wine that characterises the sum of its non-volatile substances (total dry extract), including sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matters and salts, as defined by Additional Note 4A to Chapter 22 of the CN.

new_classes.label_en: Other wine (not PDO/PGI, not variety/vintage) in containers ≤ 2 l — other (CN 2204 21 78)
new_classes.label_de: Anderer Wein (ohne g.U./g.g.A., ohne Sorte/Jahrgang), in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von 2 l oder weniger — anderer (KN 2204 21 78)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes (incl. fortified wines) and grape must in containers of 2 litres or less that does not carry a protected designation of origin (PDO/g.U.) or protected geographical indication (PGI/g.g.A.), is not a variety wine or vintage wine, is not sparkling wine (≥ 3 bar excess pressure at 20 °C) and is not semi-sparkling wine (1–3 bar), classified under CN 2204 21 78. The total dry extract is characterised by non-volatile substances including sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matters and salts (Additional Notes 4 and 5 to Chapter 22).

coverage_explanation:
The axioms capture the primary discriminating criteria for CN 2204 21 78:
(1) Container size ≤ 2 litres — covered via SmallContainer part restriction.
(2) Wine of fresh grapes production process — covered via WineFreshGrapesProduction restriction.
(3) Not sparkling wine (≥ 3 bar) — covered via complement of SparklingWineQuality.
(4) Not semi-sparkling wine (1–3 bar) — covered via complement of SemiSparklingWineQuality.
(5) Not PDO/g.U. wine — covered via complement of PDOWineQuality.
(6) Total dry extract characterisation (non-volatile substances: sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matters, salts per Additional Notes 4 and 5) — partially covered by introducing TotalDryExtractQuality and linking via has_quality; however, the numeric thresholds defined in Additional Note 4A (e.g. specific g/L ranges distinguishing dry, semi-dry, semi-sweet, sweet categories) are NOT yet encoded because no datatype property for total dry extract concentration is present in the TBox, and the note text provided does not supply the specific numeric boundaries that discriminate 22042178 from its direct siblings (22042179 etc.) solely on dry extract. 
(7) The exclusion of PGI (g.g.A.) wines, variety wines, and vintage wines that would fall under other 2204 21 subheadings is not fully formalised as complement restrictions because corresponding quality classes for PGI, variety-wine and vintage-wine are not present in the current TBox; this is noted as a coverage gap reducing the score from 1.0 to 0.72.
